notes:
Platinum-series LV.X-era set (pl2, English 2009-05-16, 111 main cards + RT1-RT6 Rotom + 3 secret Pikachu). Booster = 9 cards/pack, 36-pack box, NO ETB (etb=null) — same vintage structure as the dataset's ex-dragon-frontiers precedent: 5 common + 2 uncommon + 1 reverse-holo + 1 rare-or-better. Bulbapedia booster-pack page confirms: 1 rare + ~3 uncommon + remainder common + 1 reverse-holo per pack (international).

Buckets confirmed against data/snapshot/snapshot.json: rare-holo:13 (#1-13/111 standard holos), rare:30, uncommon:32, common:33, lv-x:9 (#103-111/111), secret-rare:3 (#112-114/111 crosshatch Pikachu). The 6 Rotom RT holos are NOT a separate catalog bucket in the snapshot, so per the integrity rule they are folded into the rare-holo band rather than inventing a bucket the set lacks.

rare-or-better slot distribution (sums to exactly 1.0):
- secret-rare 0.0278 = 1:36 packs (~1 secret Pikachu/box) — Flipside Gaming part-two reports the special-holo/secret-era rate at ~1:36.
- lv-x 0.0556 = 1:18 packs (~2 LV.X/box) — Flipside Gaming part-two: '~1:18 packs for later sets in the LV. X era' (Platinum period); corroborated by Rising Rivals community ~2/box reports. Conservative end of the cited 1:9-1:18 band; vintage rates not inflated.
- rare-holo 0.3333 = ~1:3 packs (~12/box) era holo baseline; absorbs the 6 Rotom RT holos the catalog doesn't expose.
- rare 0.5833 = remainder (plain non-holo rare).
Reverse-holo slot: neutral 50/50 common/uncommon (pools near-equal 33/32), modeled with plain ids per dataset convention.

Confidence LOW: no per-set published study; uses sourced DP/LV.X-era community/retailer averages mapped onto Rising Rivals. Sealed boxes carry a heavy collector premium so EV/booster will sit far below box price — expected, not compensated for. All keys {common, uncommon, rare, rare-holo, lv-x, secret-rare} are a strict subset of the set's available buckets; every slot sums to exactly 1.0; slot card counts total packSize=9. Config written to data/pull-rates/rising-rivals.json and passes `npm run validate:data` (58 sets, all valid).
